summary: Five distinct vulnerabilities and four CVEs were found. Three critical CVEs were auto-remediated by deterministic fixers; the SQL injection and one critical CVE were deferred to the LLM patch pass.

findings.title: Hardcoded application secret
findings.root_cause: The application.properties file contains a hardcoded `app.secret.apiKey` value that an attacker reading the repository can extract and use to bypass the API authentication layer.
findings.suggested_fix: Move the value to an environment variable; reference it via ${APP_SECRET_API_KEY}.

findings.title: SQL injection via string concatenation
findings.root_cause: Native SQL queries are built by concatenating user-supplied parameters into the SQL string, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary SQL.
findings.evidence: String sql = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = '" + name + "'";
findings.suggested_fix: Use parameterised queries: String sql = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ?"; then .setParameter(1, name).

findings.title: Plaintext password comparison
findings.root_cause: loginUnsafe() compares the supplied password to the stored hash with String.equals(), which is vulnerable to timing attacks and bypasses the bcrypt verification step.
